tasks.title: Split oversized TUI app module to reduce defect risk
tasks.description:
Principles violated: Localize change (D), KISS (A), Cohesion/SRP (D).

`crates/ralph/src/tui/app.rs` is 2,278 lines, which increases navigation cost, makes reviews harder, and raises the risk of unrelated edits colliding.

Why it matters (failure modes):
- Small UI changes require touching a large file with many responsibilities (event handling, runner integration, rendering coordination, terminal setup).
- Harder to add focused tests or reason about invariants; regressions are easier to introduce.

Minimal proposed fix:
- Split `tui/app.rs` into cohesive submodules (e.g., terminal setup, event loop, runner integration, app state mutations, rendering coordination), keeping the public API stable.
- Add module docs (`//!`) for each new file explaining responsibilities and invariants.
- Keep refactor behavior-preserving; rely on existing test suite as a safety net and add targeted unit tests for any newly isolated logic.

Acceptance criteria:
- `crates/ralph/src/tui/app.rs` reduced substantially (goal: <800 LOC) with new submodules under `crates/ralph/src/tui/`.
- No behavior changes (existing tests pass).
- `make ci` passes.
evidence: Command: `wc -l crates/ralph/src/tui/app.rs` => 2278
Command output: `wc -l crates/ralph/src/tui/app.rs crates/ralph/src/commands/run/mod.rs crates/ralph/src/config.rs` (shows app.rs as the largest production module)
plan: Identify natural seams in tui/app.rs (terminal setup, event loop, runner orchestration, state updates, rendering) and map to new modules
Extract code into new files under crates/ralph/src/tui/ (keep App type and public entrypoints stable)
Add module docs for each new module and keep responsibilities narrow
Update imports and ensure no circular dependencies are introduced
Verify by running `make ci` and confirming it passes

tasks.title: Split oversized run command module to improve maintainability
tasks.description:
Principles violated: Localize change (D), KISS (A), Cohesion/SRP (D).

`crates/ralph/src/commands/run/mod.rs` is 1,734 lines and mixes multiple responsibilities (preflight, runner/session setup, phase orchestration, supervision hooks, reporting). This increases defect risk and makes targeted improvements harder.

Why it matters (failure modes):
- Changes to one aspect of the run pipeline (e.g., session handling) require edits in a large, tightly coupled module.
- Harder to write focused tests and reason about invariants across phases.

Minimal proposed fix:
- Split `commands/run/mod.rs` into submodules (e.g., preflight, session, phases facade, reporting), keeping the CLI API stable.
- Ensure each new module has `//!` docs and clear invariants.
- Keep refactor behavior-preserving; rely on the existing run tests as a safety net.

Acceptance criteria:
- `crates/ralph/src/commands/run/mod.rs` reduced substantially (goal: <800 LOC) with extracted modules under `crates/ralph/src/commands/run/`.
- No behavior changes (existing tests pass).
- `make ci` passes.
evidence: Command: `wc -l crates/ralph/src/commands/run/mod.rs` => 1734
Makefile CI order includes run pipeline tests; refactor must remain behavior-preserving (validate via make ci)
plan: Identify cohesive groups of functions/types in commands/run/mod.rs and define target submodules
Extract code into new submodules and keep mod.rs as the thin facade
Add module docs for each new module
Run and fix any clippy/format issues caused by moves
Verify by running `make ci` and confirming it passes

tasks.title: Fix CONTRIBUTING.md CI gate step order and add drift guard
tasks.description:
Principles violated: Consistency/Integrity (J), Least astonishment (G).

`CONTRIBUTING.md` documents a CI gate step order that does not match the actual `make ci` pipeline. This is a concrete docs-code mismatch that can mislead contributors and cause churn when debugging CI failures locally.

Why it matters (failure modes):
- Contributors may run the wrong sequence when trying to reproduce failures or optimize local workflows.
- Docs drift is likely to recur unless guarded.

Minimal proposed fix:
- Update the documented pipeline order in `CONTRIBUTING.md` to match `Makefile:ci`.
- Add a small test (or extend an existing Makefile contract test) that asserts `CONTRIBUTING.md` contains the same ordered step list as the Makefile to prevent future drift.

Acceptance criteria:
- `CONTRIBUTING.md` CI step order matches Makefile `ci` recipe.
- New/updated test fails on drift and passes when aligned.
- `make ci` passes.
evidence: CONTRIBUTING.md:52-56 lists: check-env-safety → check-backup-artifacts → generate → format → type-check → lint → build → test → install
Makefile:130-139 runs: check-env-safety → check-backup-artifacts → deps → format → type-check → lint → test → build → generate → install
AGENTS.md:16 matches the Makefile order
Command: `nl -ba CONTRIBUTING.md | sed -n '40,80p'` and `nl -ba Makefile | sed -n '120,150p'`
plan: Update the CI step list in CONTRIBUTING.md to match Makefile:ci
Add/extend a test to assert the documented step sequence matches the Makefile pipeline
Verify by running `make ci` and confirming it passes

tasks.title: Fix config module docs precedence statement (defaults/global/project)
tasks.description:
Principles violated: Consistency/Integrity (J).

The module-level docs in `crates/ralph/src/config.rs` claim config merging precedence is `global → project → defaults`, which implies defaults override project config. This contradicts `docs/configuration.md` and typical layering (defaults first, then global, then project; CLI overrides both).

Why it matters:
- Misleading documentation causes incorrect future edits and misunderstandings when debugging config behavior.

Minimal proposed fix:
- Update the module docs line to reflect the real precedence order (defaults → global → project; CLI flags override both).

Acceptance criteria:
- `crates/ralph/src/config.rs` docs match `docs/configuration.md` and the actual resolution behavior.
- `make ci` passes.
evidence: crates/ralph/src/config.rs:23 says: 'Config merging follows precedence: global → project → defaults.'
docs/configuration.md:6-10 says project config takes precedence over global; CLI overrides both; defaults are schema-defined
Command: `nl -ba crates/ralph/src/config.rs | sed -n '1,40p'` and `nl -ba docs/configuration.md | sed -n '1,20p'`
plan: Update the precedence statement in crates/ralph/src/config.rs module docs
Double-check any other mentions of precedence in docs for consistency
Verify by running `make ci` and confirming it passes

tasks.title: Implement dependency-based auto-execution for unblocked tasks
tasks.description:
Tasks with depends_on don't automatically start when dependencies complete. Users must manually check and run tasks even when all prerequisites are satisfied. This creates friction and delays.

Goals:
1. Add `ralph run --auto-deps` mode that monitors done.json for dependency satisfaction
2. Automatically promote blocked tasks to todo when dependencies complete
3. Optionally auto-start tasks when dependencies done (with --start-when-ready)
4. Add TUI indicator showing 'N tasks unblocked and ready'
5. Support dependency chain visualization

Evidence:
- Current: depends_on field exists but is only validated, not monitored
- Gap: No automation for dependency lifecycle
- User pain: Manual tracking of when prerequisites complete
evidence: Current capability: Dependency validation exists - checks for cycles and missing deps (queue/dependencies.rs)
Gap observed: No automation - tasks with satisfied deps still require manual status change
Queue explain: `ralph queue explain` shows why tasks aren't runnable but doesn't auto-resolve
Field exists: Task schema has depends_on array but no auto-promotion logic
plan: Add DependencyMonitor to watch done.json for completed dependencies
Add `ralph run --auto-deps [--start-when-ready]` CLI option
Implement auto-promote logic: when dependency task completes, check dependents and promote to todo
Add 'ready to run' section in TUI showing unblocked tasks
Add notification when tasks become unblocked (desktop + webhook)
Add dependency satisfaction indicator in task list (checkmark when all deps done)
Update docs/workflow.md with dependency auto-execution patterns
Add tests for complex dependency chains and edge cases (circular protection, missing deps)
Verify by creating task A, task B depending on A, completing A, and confirming B auto-promotes to todo

custom_fields.user_value: Removes manual dependency tracking, enables automatic workflow progression, reduces delays

tasks.title: Add configuration profiles for quick workflow switching
tasks.description:
No way to quickly switch between different workflow configurations. Users must manually edit config or pass many CLI flags. Add named profiles that bundle runner, model, phases, and other settings.

Goals:
1. Add `profiles` section to config with named presets
2. Support `ralph run --profile quick-fix` or `ralph scan --profile thorough`
3. Profiles override base config but CLI flags override profiles
4. Include built-in profiles: quick (phases=1, runner=kimi), thorough (phases=3, runner=claude)
5. Add `ralph config profiles` command to list/manage profiles

Evidence:
- Current: Config has agent.* settings but no grouping mechanism
- Gap: No quick way to switch between 'quick fix' vs 'deep review' modes
- User pain: Must remember and type multiple flags: `--runner claude --model opus --phases 3 --effort high`
evidence: Current capability: All agent settings are flat - runner, model, phases, effort exist individually
Gap observed: No way to save/restore common setting combinations
CLI pattern: Users frequently use same flag combinations (--runner X --model Y --phases Z)
Precedent: Many CLI tools have profiles/contexts (kubectl, AWS CLI, Docker)
plan: Add ProfileConfig struct with all agent settings that can be overridden
Add profiles: HashMap<String, ProfileConfig> to root config
Add --profile <name> flag to run, task, scan commands
Implement profile resolution: base config → profile → CLI flags (later overrides earlier)
Add built-in profiles: 'quick' (kimi, phases=1), 'thorough' (claude/opus, phases=3), 'balanced' (sonnet, phases=2)
Add `ralph config profiles list/show/add/remove` subcommands
Add profile validation (ensure referenced profiles exist)
Update docs/configuration.md with profile examples
Verify by running `ralph run one --profile quick` and confirming correct runner/phases are used

custom_fields.user_value: Reduces CLI verbosity, enables workflow presets, improves discoverability of settings

tasks.title: Add intelligent retry with exponential backoff for transient failures
tasks.description:
Runner failures are currently terminal with no automatic retry. Network issues, rate limits, or temporary runner unavailability require manual intervention. Add smart retry classification and exponential backoff.

Goals:
1. Classify failures as retryable vs permanent vs requires-user-input
2. Add exponential backoff for retryable failures (network, rate limit, temporary unavailability)
3. Add config: max_retries (default 3), retry_backoff_base_ms (default 1000)
4. Distinguish runner-specific errors (e.g., 429 rate limit vs 401 auth failure)
5. Add retry count to task metadata and show in TUI

Evidence:
- Current: RunnerError enum has variants but no retry classification
- Gap: All failures immediately surface to user
- User pain: Transient network issues require manual restart
evidence: Current capability: RunnerError captures failure details but execution flow immediately surfaces to user
Gap observed: No retry classification - NonZeroExit, Timeout, Interrupted all treated as terminal
Error types: RunnerError::Timeout, RunnerError::Interrupted are clearly retryable
Config gap: No retry-related settings in agent config section
plan: Add FailureClassification enum: Retryable { max_attempts, backoff }, NonRetryable, RequiresUserInput
Implement classify_error() for each runner type based on exit codes and error patterns
Add retry_attempts counter to task metadata (custom_fields or new field)
Add retry config to agent: max_retries (3), retry_backoff_base_ms (1000), retry_backoff_multiplier (2.0)
Implement retry loop in run execution with exponential backoff sleep
Add retry indicator in TUI execution view ('Retry 2/3 in 4s...')
Add webhook event for task_retry with attempt count
Update docs/error-handling.md with retry behavior documentation
Add tests for retry logic including backoff calculation and max retry exhaustion
Verify by simulating network failure and confirming automatic retry with backoff

custom_fields.user_value: Reduces manual intervention for transient failures, improves success rate, handles network hiccups

tasks.title: Implement continuous execution mode with background daemon capability
tasks.description:
No way to run Ralph as a persistent background service. Watch mode is foreground-only, and run loop stops when queue empties. Add continuous execution that monitors queue and auto-executes tasks.

Goals:
1. Add `ralph run --continuous` that loops indefinitely, sleeping when queue empty
2. Add `ralph daemon` command to run as background service (Unix socket/Windows service)
3. Support graceful shutdown with signal handling
4. Add queue-watching trigger (re-check when queue.json changes)
5. Support configurable idle sleep interval (default 30s)

Evidence:
- Current: `ralph run loop` stops when no tasks; `ralph watch` is foreground only
- Gap: No background service capability
- User pain: Must keep terminal open or use external tools (screen, systemd)
evidence: Current capability: `ralph run loop` exists but exits when queue empty
Gap observed: No continuous mode - users must manually restart or use external process managers
Watch mode: `ralph watch` only watches files, not queue, and is foreground-only
Signal handling: signal.rs exists but only for graceful stop, not daemon lifecycle
plan: Add --continuous flag to run command with configurable --idle-interval-secs (default 30)
Implement file watcher on queue.json to trigger immediate re-check when queue changes
Add `ralph daemon start/stop/status` commands with PID file management
Implement Unix socket for daemon communication (status, pause, resume)
Add daemon logs to .ralph/logs/daemon.log with rotation
Add systemd service file template in docs/ or scripts/
Add TUI 'Connect to daemon' mode for monitoring background execution
Update docs with daemon setup instructions
Add tests for daemon lifecycle including graceful shutdown
Verify by running `ralph daemon start`, adding a task, and confirming it auto-executes

custom_fields.user_value: Enables 'set and forget' operation, integrates with system services, reduces manual monitoring

tasks.title: Add ETA accuracy tracking and prediction improvement
tasks.description:
ETA estimates are provided but never validated against actual completion times. No feedback loop to improve predictions. Add accuracy tracking to measure and improve ETA predictions over time.

Goals:
1. Track predicted vs actual completion times for each task
2. Calculate ETA accuracy metrics (MAE, bias, variance by runner/model)
3. Add `ralph productivity accuracy` command showing prediction performance
4. Use accuracy data to adjust future predictions (calibration)
5. Show confidence intervals, not just point estimates

Evidence:
- Current: ETA calculator exists but only computes weighted averages
- Gap: No accuracy measurement or feedback loop
- User pain: ETAs may be consistently optimistic/pessimistic but never corrected
evidence: Current capability: ETA calculator computes weighted averages by (runner, model, phase_count)
Gap observed: No comparison of predicted vs actual times - predictions never improve
Execution history: Stores phase durations but not the ETA that was predicted
Confidence levels: High/Medium/Low exist but based only on sample count, not accuracy
plan: Add predicted_duration_ms to execution history entries (captured at task start)
Add accuracy metrics to productivity data: mae_ms, bias_ms, variance_ms by (runner, model)
Implement accuracy calculation: compare predicted vs actual at task completion
Add calibration factor that adjusts future predictions based on historical bias
Add `ralph productivity accuracy [--runner X] [--days 30]` command
Show confidence intervals in TUI: 'ETA: 5-8 minutes (85% confidence)'
Add accuracy webhook events for external monitoring
Update docs with ETA accuracy interpretation guide
Add tests for calibration algorithm edge cases (insufficient data, outliers)
Verify by running `ralph productivity accuracy` after completing several tasks and seeing metrics

custom_fields.user_value: Improves planning accuracy, builds trust in ETAs, enables better time management

tasks.title: Add AI-powered task complexity analysis with auto-split suggestions
tasks.description:
No automated assistance for determining if a task is too large or should be split. Users must manually judge complexity. Add AI analysis that evaluates task scope/plan and suggests splitting when appropriate.

Goals:
1. Add `ralph task analyze <TASK_ID>` command for complexity evaluation
2. Analyze: scope length, plan step count, evidence detail, estimated effort
3. Suggest splitting when complexity exceeds thresholds
4. Generate split proposals with logical task boundaries
5. Integrate with existing `ralph task split` command

Evidence:
- Current: Manual task splitting exists (`ralph task split`) but no guidance on when to use
- Gap: No complexity heuristics or automated analysis
- User pain: Large tasks often fail or take much longer than expected
evidence: Current capability: `ralph task split` exists but is manual - no guidance on when to split
Gap observed: No complexity scoring or automated suggestions
Refactor task builder: Exists for large files but not for task complexity
Task schema: Has scope, evidence, plan fields that could be analyzed
plan: Create TaskComplexityAnalyzer with heuristics: scope word count, plan step count, evidence quality
Add complexity_score (1-10) and complexity_reasoning to task custom_fields
Implement `ralph task analyze <TASK_ID> [--suggest-split]` command
Add --analyze flag to `ralph task build` to check complexity before creating
Integrate with split command: `ralph task split --auto-suggest` uses AI to propose boundaries
Add complexity warning in TUI when selecting high-complexity tasks
Add webhook event for complexity_analysis_complete
Update task builder prompt to encourage right-sized tasks
Add tests for complexity scoring edge cases (short scope but complex plan)
Verify by running `ralph task analyze RQ-XXXX` on a large task and receiving split suggestions

custom_fields.user_value: Prevents oversized tasks, improves success rate, reduces manual judgment

tasks.title: Implement incoming webhook server for external integrations
tasks.description:
Ralph can SEND webhooks but cannot RECEIVE them. No HTTP endpoint for external services (GitHub, Slack, CI/CD) to trigger actions. Add an incoming webhook server for external integration.

Goals:
1. Add `ralph server` command that starts HTTP server on configurable port (default 8473)
2. Support endpoints: POST /webhook/task (create task), POST /webhook/trigger (start run)
3. Add HMAC-SHA256 signature verification for security
4. Support GitHub webhook format for issue/PR events
5. Add request queuing and rate limiting

Evidence:
- Current: Outbound webhooks exist (9 event types) but no inbound
- Gap: No HTTP server capability
- User pain: Can't integrate with external CI systems, GitHub webhooks, Slack commands
evidence: Current capability: Outbound webhook system is mature (9 events, retries, HMAC)
Gap observed: No incoming webhook capability - can't receive external triggers
No HTTP server: Main is purely CLI, no server modules exist
Integration gap: GitHub webhooks, Slack commands, CI triggers not possible
plan: Add axum or warp dependency for HTTP server (lightweight, async)
Create server module with configurable bind address and port (default 127.0.0.1:8473)
Implement POST /webhook/task endpoint for external task creation
Implement POST /webhook/trigger endpoint to start run loop
Add HMAC-SHA256 verification using configured webhook.secret
Add GitHub-compatible endpoint at /webhook/github for PR/issue events
Implement request queue with rate limiting (requests per minute per source)
Add server logging and request/response logging to .ralph/logs/server.log
Add `ralph server start/stop/status` CLI commands
Create example integrations for GitHub webhooks and Slack commands
Verify by running `ralph server start`, then POST to /webhook/task and confirming task created

custom_fields.user_value: Enables external integrations, CI triggers, GitHub/Slack commands, third-party automation

tasks.title: Add smart scheduling rules for time-based task automation
tasks.description:
No time-based automation rules. Can't automatically escalate priority, reject stale drafts, or change status based on age. Add configurable rules for time-based task actions.

Goals:
1. Add `automation_rules` config section with time-based triggers
2. Support rules: escalate priority after N days, auto-reject stale drafts, remind after delay
3. Add `ralph queue rules` command to list/apply rules
4. Support dry-run mode to preview rule effects
5. Integrate with continuous/daemon mode for automatic application

Evidence:
- Current: Basic `scheduled_start` field exists but no rule engine
- Gap: No automation based on task age or time in status
- User pain: Stale drafts accumulate, high-priority work gets forgotten
evidence: Current capability: `ralph task schedule` sets future start date - manual only
Gap observed: No recurring or conditional automation based on task state
Queue prune: Exists for age-based deletion but not general rules
Aging report: Would identify stale tasks but not act on them
plan: Add AutomationRule struct with trigger (time_in_status, age), condition, and action
Add automation_rules: Vec<AutomationRule> to config
Implement rule engine that evaluates rules against queue on schedule/trigger
Add `ralph queue rules list/test/apply` CLI commands
Support actions: set_priority, set_status, add_tag, add_note, notify
Add built-in rule templates: 'escalate_after_7d', 'reject_stale_drafts'
Integrate with daemon mode for automatic rule application
Add webhook events for rule_triggered and rule_action_applied
Add TUI notification when rules are applied to tasks
Add tests for rule evaluation including complex conditions
Verify by configuring a rule 'after 1d in draft, add tag stale', waiting 1 day, and confirming tag added

custom_fields.user_value: Prevents forgotten work, automates queue hygiene, reduces manual status management

tasks.title: Add plugin marketplace with remote install and discovery
tasks.description:
Plugins can only be installed from local directories. No discovery, versioning, or remote installation. Add a plugin marketplace for easy sharing and installation.

Goals:
1. Add `ralph plugin search <query>` to find plugins from registry
2. Add `ralph plugin install <source>` supporting git URLs, GitHub repos, registry IDs
3. Add version resolution and update checking
4. Add plugin registry format (JSON index)
5. Support signed plugins for security

Evidence:
- Current: `ralph plugin install <local-dir>` only supports local paths
- Gap: No remote installation, no discovery mechanism
- User pain: Must manually download/clone plugins, no version management
evidence: Current capability: Plugin install only supports local directory paths (commands/plugin/mod.rs)
Gap observed: No remote install, no version management, no discovery
Registry exists: PluginRegistry but only for discovered local plugins
Discovery: Only scans ~/.config/ralph/plugins/ and .ralph/plugins/

Extend `ralph plugin install` to support: github:user/repo, https://git..., registry:plugin-id

tasks.title: Implement bidirectional GitHub sync for issues and PRs
tasks.description:
GitHub integration is one-way only (export/publish). Tasks can become GitHub issues but status doesn't sync back. Add bidirectional synchronization.

Goals:
1. Add `ralph queue sync github` command to sync task status with linked issues/PRs
2. Poll GitHub for status changes (issue closed → task done, PR merged → task done)
3. Sync comments bidirectionally
4. Support labels sync (GitHub labels ↔ tags)
5. Add webhook support for real-time sync (with ralph server feature)

Evidence:
- Current: `ralph queue issue publish` creates GitHub issues but no sync
- Gap: One-way only - no status sync back from GitHub
- User pain: Must manually mark tasks done when GitHub issues close
evidence: Current capability: `ralph queue issue publish` and parallel mode PR creation exist
Gap observed: No synchronization - GitHub issue close doesn't update task status
Export only: Export formats include GitHub markdown but no import/sync
Git module: issue.rs and pr.rs exist but only have create/update, no sync polling

Implement GitHub sync polling using gh CLI or GraphQL API
Add `ralph queue sync github [--poll-interval 5m]` command

Add label/tag sync configuration (map GitHub labels to Ralph tags)
Integrate with ralph server for webhook-based real-time sync
Add sync status indicator in TUI showing last sync time
Add dry-run mode to preview sync changes
Update docs with GitHub sync setup and configuration
Add tests for sync logic including conflict resolution
Verify by publishing task to GitHub, closing issue, running sync, and confirming task marked done

custom_fields.user_value: Eliminates manual status sync, keeps GitHub and Ralph in sync, enables hybrid workflows

tasks.title: Add health check system with disk space and resource monitoring
tasks.description:
No proactive health monitoring. Queue operations, temp files, and debug logs can consume disk space without warning. Add health checks that warn before resources are exhausted.

Goals:

2. Add `ralph doctor --health-check` for continuous monitoring
3. Configurable thresholds: disk_space_threshold_mb (default 1000)
4. Add health status to TUI status bar
5. Add webhook alerts for health issues

Evidence:
- Current: `ralph doctor` runs one-time checks but no continuous monitoring
- Gap: No resource monitoring or alerts
- Risk: Disk full during long runs can cause data corruption
evidence: Current capability: `ralph doctor` validates environment and runner binaries
Gap observed: No disk space, memory, or resource monitoring

Temp files: Parallel mode creates workspaces with no cleanup on disk full

tasks.title: Add undo/redo system for task operations
tasks.description:
No way to undo task operations. Accidental deletes, status changes, or edits can't be easily reverted. Add an undo/redo system for queue mutations.

Goals:
1. Add operation history stack for queue mutations
2. Support undo for: delete, archive, status change, edit, batch operations
3. Add `ralph queue undo` and `ralph queue redo` commands
4. Add undo support in TUI (Ctrl+Z / Ctrl+Y)
5. Persist undo history across sessions (configurable)

Evidence:
- Current: No undo capability - operations are immediate and permanent
- Gap: Accidental changes require manual repair or queue.json editing
- User pain: One wrong `ralph task delete` or batch operation can lose work
evidence: Current capability: Queue repair exists for corruption but not for user mistakes
Gap observed: No operation history or undo capability anywhere
Batch operations: Can affect many tasks at once with no recovery path
Delete: `ralph task delete` immediately removes task from queue
plan: Add OperationRecord struct capturing operation type, before/after state, timestamp
Add undo_history: Vec<OperationRecord> to queue with max size limit (default 100)
Instrument all queue mutation operations to push to undo stack
Implement `ralph queue undo [--steps N]` to revert last N operations
Implement `ralph queue redo` to re-apply undone operations
Add Ctrl+Z / Ctrl+Y keybindings in TUI for undo/redo
Add undo indicator in TUI showing 'Undo available (3 operations)'
Persist undo history to .ralph/cache/undo_history.json (optional, default on)
Add config option: max_undo_history_size, persist_undo_history
Update docs with undo/redo usage and limitations
Add tests for undo/redo including complex sequences and edge cases
Verify by running `ralph task delete RQ-XXXX`, then `ralph queue undo`, and confirming task restored

custom_fields.user_value: Reduces fear of mistakes, enables experimentation, improves data safety
